title: rubus × subplicatus
intro:
Rubus × subplicatus, also known as the hybrid black raspberry, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the yellow raspberry (Rubus idaeus), resulting in a unique plant with characteristics from both parent species.

The medicinal properties of Rubus × subplicatus include its high content of anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and other antioxidants, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

The health benefits of consuming Rubus × subplicatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer, as well as its ability to support digestive health and immune function.

Rubus × subplicatus can be consumed as a fresh fruit, used in jams and preserves, or prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
